prompt:
Every team here has hand-rolled retry loops around S3 uploads and the ones I've audited all have at least one bug (sleeping after the last attempt, retrying auth failures forever, ignoring ctx). I want one blessed helper: package retry, root of module go-retry, stdlib only.

API:

  type Backoff func(attempt int) time.Duration   // attempt is 1-based: delay taken AFTER attempt n fails
  func Constant(d time.Duration) Backoff
  func Exponential(base, max time.Duration) Backoff  // base, 2*base, 4*base... clamped to max

  type Options struct {
      MaxAttempts int                                        // total attempts incl. the first; < 1 is an error
      Backoff     Backoff                                    // nil => retry with no delay
      Retryable   func(error) bool                           // nil => every error is retryable
      Sleep       func(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error // nil => real timer-based sleep that aborts on ctx
  }
  func Do(ctx context.Context, opts Options, fn func(ctx context.Context) error) error

Behavior I care about:
- success returns nil immediately, zero sleeps on a first-try success
- between failed attempts sleep exactly Backoff(attempt); NEVER sleep after the final attempt
- if Retryable returns false for an error, stop right there and hand that error back unwrapped-enough that errors.Is finds it
- when all attempts are burned, return an error that wraps the LAST attempt's error (errors.Is must see it) and whose message states the count like "3 attempts"
- ctx already canceled => don't even run fn once, return the ctx error; ctx canceled during a backoff sleep => return the ctx error and never start another attempt
- Do(..., nil fn) and MaxAttempts < 1 are option errors, fn must not run

retry_test.go is the acceptance contract — read it, do not modify it. Tests inject Sleep so nothing depends on wall time, but the nil-Sleep default has to actually work too. Green = go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
